title: elongatia janetiae
intro: Elongatia janetiae, commonly known as Janet's waltzing matilda or Elkhorn fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The therapeutic actions of Elongatia janetiae are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Elongatia janetiae can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. In some cases, ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause neurological symptoms, including headaches, dizziness, and tremors. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can also cause skin burns and blisters.
